mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and relaxes financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing. The signal is falsified if rate cuts are not followed by higher short-horizon equity returns than non-cut periods.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after months with declining FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the average effect is negative in a rolling 3-year window, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent local trend indicates worsening labor market stress and higher recession probability, which should pressure equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The claim fails if labor deterioration is not followed by weaker forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances of rising UNRATE are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST drops sharply while RSAFS softens, the combination signals broad demand deceleration across housing and retail activity, which should lead investors to mark down cyclical earnings and depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if either series weakens alone but the combined demand slowdown does not matter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of declining HOUST and declining RSAFS does not produce lower 60-day forward SPX returns than periods when only one or neither series is weakening, or if the interaction term has no stable negative sign in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with contained CPIAUCSL implies stronger real output without an inflation shock, which supports margins and earnings expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if stronger industrial production is accompanied by accelerating inflation that overwhelms the growth benefit.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average while CPIAUCSL inflation remains below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if periods meeting the real-growth-without-inflation trigger do not outperform other periods on 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect becomes negative when CPIAUCSL trend accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level relative to recent history tightens liquidity conditions and raises the hurdle rate for risk assets, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days. The claim is specific to restrictive policy states and fails when rates are already low or unchanged.
failure_condition: Reject if, when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, 5-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average or if the hit rate of negative returns is not materially above 50% over a full walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE indicates broadening payroll growth and improving labor slack, which should support consumer spending and equity earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The hypothesis fails if payroll gains do not coincide with declining unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE do not deliver above-average 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect and the joint condition adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 signals slower aggregate real demand and weaker future corporate revenues, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is falsified if GDP slowdowns are not followed by weak equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 deceleration is not followed by negative or below-median 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the rolling 3-year correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns turns persistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: A housing downturn combined with weak retail sales signals a broad consumer-demand retrenchment that should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month through lower cyclical earnings expectations. The signal is falsified if either housing weakness or retail weakness appears alone but the joint pattern has no extra effect.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average and RSAFS growth turns negative over the same period
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-retail contraction trigger does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative and significant in repeated out-of-sample tests.
